titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Maximilian Tedesco Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michelle Peralta

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 26, 2024 at 07:05

descriptivenote.p: The materials are in German, French, and English.

did.abstract: The materials in the Paul Maximilian Tedesco papers fall mainly into three areas: documents relating to Tedesco's academic qualifications and performance evaluation as a secondary school teacher in Vienna; letters of reference and support for his scholarly work for research leave and for academic positions; and material relating to his forced retirement from teaching after the annexation of Austria (1938), papers permitting emigration, and eventual appointment to the Institute for Advanced Study in 1938.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials in this collection fall mainly into three areas: documents relating to Paul Tedesco's academic qualifications and performance evaluation as a secondary school teacher in Vienna; letters of reference and support for his scholarly work for research leave and for academic positions; and material relating to his forced retirement from teaching after the Anschluss (1938), papers permitting emigration, and eventual appointment to the Institute for Advanced Study in 1938.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Estate of Stanley Isler, 2019.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Paul Maximilian Tedesco was born in Vienna, Austria on May 5, 1898. He studied Indo-Europoean languages with Paule Kretschmer, Indology with Leopold von Schroeder, and Iranian studies at the Unviersity of Vienna under Bernhard Geiger. Tedesco earned his doctorate in 1920.

Although Tedesco had the support of scholars such as Geiger, Antoine Meillet, and Friedrich Carl Andreas who recommended and supported Tedesco as a scholar in Iranian studies, he was unable to secure an academic appointment. The rejection of his applications was under the pretext of his qualifications, which was not uncommon for Jewish scholars. Instead, Tedesco taught at various secondary schools in Vienna beginning in 1925. He was on a research leave when he was forced into retirement after the annexation of Austria into Germany by the Nazi regime (Anschluss) in 1938.

Tedesco then left Austria and immigrated to the United States. He spent his early years in the U.S. at the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton, New Jersey before moving to New Haven, Connecticut in 1943 for a research fellowship at Yale Unviersity. Tedesco eventually became an associate professor of Indo-Iranian and Slavic Languages in 1952. From 1960 until his retirement in 1966, Tedesco was the Edward E. Salisbury Professor of Sanskrit and Comparative Philology.
He died in New Haven, Connecticut on December 17, 1980.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Maximilian Tedesco Papers (MS 2109). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts and Archives staff arranged and described these materials according to national and local standards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains: Heimatschein (domicile certificate) dated 25 April 1916; Copy of Tedesco's doctoral diploma in Latin, dated 30 June 1920 Vienna; Letter in French dated 13 August 1929 from Antoine Meillet attesting to Tedesco's qualifications; Letter in German dated 19 June 1924 presumably from Christian Bartholomae that acknowledges receipt of work and grants Tedesco permission to list him as a reference; Typed Gutachten dated 8 September 1929 from Professor Andreas (presumably Friedrich Carl Andreas) that provides a positive recommendation to the London School of Oriental Languages, attesting to Tedesco's qualifications for an open position; Materials relating to Tedesco's qualifications, appointment, performance, and compensation as a secondary school teacher in Vienna from 1921-1931; Testament to Tedesco's scholarly work from the director of the seminar for Slavic philology at the University of Vienna dated 1929 with support of Tedesco's request to renew his research leave.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains: Tedesco's Austrian identity card; Scholarly letters of reference dated 1936; Scholarly letters supporting further research leave dated 1937; Official notification of research leave dated 4 September 1937; Vienna Board of Education (Stadtschulrat) notification of forced permanent retirement dated 1938 May 30; Handwritten copy of Tedesco's birth certificate dated 1938; bound together materials, including a statement that Tedesco' owes no taxes or fees and has permission to emigrate dated 11 August 1938, notification of appointment regarding tax statement, statement for military administration confirming Tedesco as Jewish ("Volljude"); Letter of invitation to join the Institute for Advanced Study at Princeton, New Jersey from Director Abraham Flexner dated 8 July 1938; Certificate attesting to Tedesco as Jewish for military administration; Personal letter from Abraham Flexner with suggestions for how to start work at the Institute for Advanced Study.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Offprint, signed by Tedesco to Warren Cowgill (Yale Linguistics Professor), "Studien zur Sprachwissenschaft und Kulturkunde. Gedenkschrift für Wilhelm Brandenstein." Innsbruck, 1968.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains: A reference letter from Professor Arthur Christensen of the University of Copenhagen.
